expectations.text: The output includes a valid JSON animation plan with scene, objects, motions, timeline, camera, and style fields
expectations.evidence: output.md contains a complete JSON plan with all required top-level fields: scene, objects, motions, timeline, camera, style, render_settings

expectations.text: The output includes a complete Blender Python script using the bpy module
expectations.evidence: bouncing_ball.py is a 124-line script importing bpy with full scene setup

expectations.text: The script creates a UV sphere with a red material
The script includes keyframe animation for bouncing (location changes on Z-axis)
expectations.evidence: bounce_data dict with 8 keyframes showing Z-axis location changes from 5.0 down to 0.5 and back

expectations.text: The script includes squash-and-stretch via scale keyframes
expectations.evidence: bounce_data includes scale values like (1.3, 1.3, 0.6) for squash and (0.85, 0.85, 1.2) for stretch

expectations.text: The script sets up a static side-view camera
expectations.evidence: camera_add(location=(10, 0, 3)) with rotation_euler pointing at the ball — X=10 gives a clear side view
Three-point lighting: SUN key light, AREA fill light, POINT rim light

expectations.text: The script includes render output settings
The output includes instructions for how to run the script
expectations.evidence: output.md has a 'How to Run' section with blender --background --python commands and --render flag
